programs.name: Soccer: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: Virtual Adapted Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.Virtual recreation allows you to participate in activities without being on-site and from any space you choose.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Club: Reading
programs.description: If you love books and reading, you will love this activity. Not only will you enjoy your favourite book, but you can share ideas and thoughts about your reading with others. This activity is facilitated by staff, there is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects.

programs.name: Creative Writing Comic Books/Anime
programs.description: Explore your powers of observation and stretch your imagination through creative writing. As you learn about writing components like structure, character development and theme, you will incorporate them into short stories, fantasy and poetry you can share.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet

programs.name: Dance: Caribbean
programs.description: Let rhythm and music move you with Caribbean dance! Focusing on fluidity and expression, learn Soca and Jazz-style movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.
programs.location: Gord and Irene Risk Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Dance: K-Pop
programs.description: Korean pop, or K-pop, is a music genre that has inspired its own dance style, and this is your chance to learn it. Blending classic dance moves from jazz and hip-hop with Korean pop sounds, you will learn basic routines through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Latin
programs.description: Calling all Latin dance lovers, this is your chance to try the tango, rumba, cha-cha and mambo. Learn basic steps, postures and holds through various exercises and engaging choreography.  Partners are not required as dancers will be partnered in this class as needed.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts. Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.* understand proper etiquette and simple classroom rules* Demonstrate fundamental martial arts skills (e.g. blocks,strikes,kicks,stance,footwork)* Demonstrate simple fighting skills* Develop tactical concepts of space and distance* Show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4

programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. In this introduction to cooking, you will learn about healthy foods, the importance of safety and practice basic skills like mixing and measuring with easy-to-follow recipes that taste great.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. programs.name: Learn To Weight Train - Small Group
programs.description: Strengthen your muscles with weight training. Perfect for beginners, you will learn fundamental techniques and a variety of exercises to help enhance your strength, mobility, flexibility and stamina safely. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Stroke Improvement
programs.description: Work on stroke correction and improving technique through drills and progressions focused on front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Tiny Tots Swim
programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Crafts: Diamond Dots
programs.description: Create dazzling artwork, one dot at a time! Using tiny, sparkly "diamonds," you'll place each one onto a pre-printed adhesive canvas to build beautiful, shimmering designs. Diamond dotting is a fun and relaxing activity that encourages patience, focus, and creativity. Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned crafter, you'll love watching your masterpiece come to life, dot by dot!

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water and improve your swimming skills. Develop front and back crawl, elementary backstroke and staying afloat in water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick, surface dives and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Youth 1.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. As you continue to practice and develop skills, you will work towards performing a routine at the end of the session.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private

To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water and improve your swimming skills. Develop front and back crawl, elementary backstroke and staying afloat in water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick, surface dives and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Youth 1.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. As you continue to practice and develop skills, you will work towards performing a routine at the end of the session.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1 - Small Group
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Class size is limited to four participants. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Virtual Adapted Science
programs.description: Does science excite you? If so, come explore science through hands-on activities and conduct fun and exciting experiments.Virtual recreation allows you to participate in activities without being on-site and from any space you choose.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1 - Small Group
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Create Your Space
programs.description: Create Your Space is an arts program which provides an opportunity for participants to learn and apply skills to create and animate theme based settings. Learn how to take an area (a room, hallway, lobby) and set the stage by creating it into a welcoming area in a community centre. It would also give youth the opportunity to be involved in the overall look of the Community Centre.

programs.name: Creative Calm: Drawing for Well-Being
programs.description:
Explore
drawing as a way to slow down, reflect, and reconnect with your creativity.
Through a series of guided mark-making and drawing exercises, participants will
experiment with line, texture, and simple materials while focusing on the
present moment. This relaxed and supportive class encourages curiosity,
imagination, and the joy of creating—no prior art experience required.

programs.name: Creative Clay
programs.description: Explore the possibilities of creating with clay and learn basic pottery techniques such as pinching, coiling, slab building and glazing. Each project stimulates the imagination while developing pottery skills.

programs.name: Creative Writing
programs.description: Explore your powers of observation and stretch your imagination through creative writing. As you learn about writing components like structure, character development and theme, you will incorporate them into short stories, fantasy and poetry you can share.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 (Girls)
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. This activity is for all girls.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 (Girls)
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. This activity is for all girls.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 - Small Group
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1 (Women)
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres. This activity is for all women.

programs.name: Badminton: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In badminton, a racquet sport that focuses on hand-eye coordination and has you using your racquet to rally for points with a ‘birdie’ you will learn basics like grip, serve and swing, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Cyanotype for Beginners (9-13 years)
programs.description: Get hands-on and learn all the cool steps, processes, and methods to create unique Cyanotype prints or photograms. This process involves using light to create photographic prints. Let your imagination run wild as you experiment with different materials, found objects and discover endless ways to make your artwork pop! Materials included. Special one day workshops are also available for March Break.

programs.name: Cyanotype Printing for Beginners
programs.description: Create one of a kind Cyanotype prints or photograms in this introductory photo printing workshop. Students will learn the step-by-step processes and methods needed to make these special prints. Explore the endless possibilities of imagery creation through experimentation with materials and found objects. This workshop requires an additional $20 material fee (cash only) payable to instructor. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Cycle Fit
programs.description: Whether you are new to indoor cycling or want to continue to enhance your cycling skills, Cycle Fit has something for everyone. Instruction will cover indoor cycling basics as well as how to increase intensity, keep in proper alignment and maximize fitness while reducing the risk of injury.

programs.name: Cycling: Instructional - Advanced (On the Road)
programs.description: If you are an experienced bicycle rider this is a great way to get ready for the open road. Through both guided practice and instruction, you will learn how to ride skillfully following road rules and communicating with drivers, traffic theory and most importantly, how to cycle safely. Guided rides take place on quiet streets. Equipment: You must bring your own CSA approved bike helmet and a bicycle with a working bell and a bike lock.

programs.name: Cycling: Instructional - Beginner with Caregiver
programs.description: If you are a brand-new or beginner bicycle rider, this is a fun way to gain confidence and experience on a bicycle. Learn basic skills including balance, pedalling, steering and stopping all while remaining on-site. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.Equipment: You must bring your own CSA approved bike helmet and a bicycle with a working bell and a bike lock
